phrases.intent_en: Comment on the beautiful bluegrass landscape
phrases.text_local: Quel paysage magnifique ! L'herbe bleue est vraiment extraordinaire.
phrases.pronunciation: kel pay-ee-ZAHJ mah-nyee-FEEK ! lair-buh BLUH ay vray-MON ex-tra-or-dee-NAIR
phrases.cultural_note: Kentucky bluegrass ('l'herbe bleue') is famous worldwide. The rolling green pastures of the Bluegrass Region genuinely resemble the Normandy and Haras du Pin horse country of northern France — French visitors are often struck by the similarity.

phrases.intent_en: Say you came specifically to see the horses
phrases.text_local: Je suis venu ici exprès pour voir les chevaux.
phrases.pronunciation: juh swee vuh-NOO ee-SEE ex-PRAY poor VWAR lay shuh-VOH
phrases.cultural_note: Kentucky and Normandy (home of Haras du Pin, the 'Versailles of horses') share a deep horse-breeding culture. Saying you came for the horses signals a shared passion that resonates deeply in French equestrian culture.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the farm's famous stallions
phrases.text_local: Pouvez-vous me parler des étalons célèbres élevés ici ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-VAY-voo muh par-LAY day zay-ta-LON say-LEB-ruh ay-luh-VAY ee-SEE
phrases.cultural_note: Three Chimneys is famous for standing stallions including champions descended from secretariat. 'Étalon' (stallion) is a word French gave to horse-racing English — using it in French closes the circle beautifully.

responses.french: Nous avons eu l'honneur d'élever certains des plus grands champions du monde.
responses.english: We have had the honor of breeding some of the greatest champions in the world.
responses.phonetic: noo za-VON ooh lo-NEUR day-luh-VAY sair-TAN day ploo gron SHOM-pyon doo MOND

phrases.intent_en: Ask if tours of the farm are available
phrases.text_local: Est-il possible de visiter le haras ? Y a-t-il des visites organisées ?
phrases.pronunciation: ay-TEEL po-SEE-bluh duh vee-zee-TAY luh ah-RAH ? ee ah-TEEL day vee-ZEET or-gah-nee-ZAY
phrases.cultural_note: 'Haras' is the French word for a stud farm or horse breeding estate — the same word used for the famous Haras Nationaux of France. Using it here immediately elevates the conversation and shows cultural awareness.

responses.french: Oui, des visites guidées sont proposées le matin sur réservation.
responses.english: Yes, guided tours are offered in the morning by reservation.
responses.phonetic: wee, day vee-ZEET gee-DAY son pro-po-ZAY luh ma-TAN soor ray-zair-va-SYON

responses.french: Nous organisons des visites privées pour les groupes.
responses.english: We organize private tours for groups.
responses.phonetic: noo zor-gah-nee-ZON day vee-ZEET pree-VAY poor lay GROOP

phrases.intent_en: Say Kentucky looks just like Normandy
phrases.text_local: Ce paysage me rappelle la Normandie. C'est frappant comme ressemblance !
phrases.pronunciation: suh pay-ee-ZAHJ muh ra-PEL lah nor-mon-DEE. say fra-PON kom ruh-som-BLON
phrases.cultural_note: The comparison is historically apt — both Normandy and the Kentucky Bluegrass Region have rich soils, rolling hills, and world-class horse-breeding traditions. French visitors to Kentucky genuinely make this observation.

responses.french: C'est vrai ! Beaucoup de visiteurs français font la même remarque.
responses.english: That's true! Many French visitors make the same observation.
responses.phonetic: say VRAY ! boh-KOO duh vee-zee-TEUR fron-SAY fon lah MEM ruh-MARK

phrases.intent_en: Ask what breed of horses are raised here
phrases.text_local: Quelle race de chevaux est-ce qu'on élève ici ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel RASS duh shuh-VOH ess kon ay-LEV ee-SEE
phrases.cultural_note: Thoroughbreds ('pur-sangs') dominate Kentucky horse country. The French word 'pur-sang' (pure blood) is what gave the English language the word 'thoroughbred' — another deep linguistic connection between France and Kentucky horse culture.

responses.french: Nous élevons exclusivement des pur-sangs anglais.
responses.phonetic: noo ZAY-luh-von ex-kloo-zeev-MON day poor-SON on-GLAY
